mnemonics.title: Dual Construction Steps
mnemonics.content: FVP: Face → Vertex → Pair faces by edges

mnemonics.title: Primal-Dual Relationship
mnemonics.content: DUAL MAGIC: Dual's vertices = Primal's faces; Dual's edges = Primal's edges; Dual's faces = Primal's vertices

mnemonics.title: Bridges become Loops
mnemonics.content: BLiP: Bridge becomes Loop in dual

acronyms.usage: Construction method: Place Vertex in each Face, Pair adjacent faces by edges
Bridges in primal become Loops in dual
Cycles in primal ↔ Cut-sets in dual

visualTips.visualization: Imagine each face as a room, place a light bulb (vertex) in each room, then connect bulbs whose rooms share a door (edge)
Like a mirror: if you take a picture and its reflection, they look the same - W4 wheel graphs have this property
Think of a bridge as a wall with the same landscape on both sides - in dual, this becomes a door that leads back to where you started (loop)
Face boundary = vertex degree: count the walls around a room to know how many connections the light bulb has

analogies.analogy: Think of countries on a map: each country is a face, put a capital city in each country, then draw highways between capitals of neighboring countries - that's the dual!
Like casting shadows: faces become vertices, boundaries between faces become edges connecting vertices
A square reflected in a mirror looks like the same square - self-dual graphs look the same after the dual transformation
